name: U.S. Small Business Administration - San Diego
description_short: U.S. Small Business Administration San Diego District Office provides government-backed small business funding, counseling, and federal contracting support for entrepreneurs in San Diego and Imperial counties.
description_long:
The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) San Diego District Office is a federal government agency serving the two southernmost counties in California. Established as part of the national SBA infrastructure, this office operates as a resource center for small business owners seeking financing, guidance, and government contracting opportunities. The district office is located at 550 West C St., Suite 550 in San Diego and is staffed by government employees including Acting District Director Mike Sovacool.

The San Diego SBA office provides comprehensive support across multiple business needs including SBA-backed loan programs (such as 504 loans for equipment and facilities), business counseling services, federal contracting certifications, and disaster recovery assistance. They also connect small business owners with partner organizations, lenders, and community groups that support business success. The office facilitates access to both direct SBA resources and connections to vetted third-party providers in the region.

What distinguishes this office is its role as a government-backed, non-lending intermediary that connects entrepreneurs directly to SBA funding programs and certified lenders rather than making loans directly. The office operates during standard business hours (Monday-Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.) and offers appointment-based consultations. They actively host educational events and webinars on topics like business model development and marketing for veteran-owned businesses, and maintain documented success stories of businesses that used SBA-backed financing to scale operations.

As a government office, the SBA San Diego District provides free or low-cost advisory services but does not originate loans themselves—entrepreneurs must work with SBA-approved lenders for actual financing. Service availability depends on office capacity and hours of operation. While the office provides valuable connections and guidance, approval for SBA-backed loans still requires meeting lender and SBA underwriting criteria.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Government-backed SBA loan programs including 504 loans for facility and equipment financing
Free business counseling and advisory services through district office staff
Direct connections to SBA-approved lenders and community partner organizations
Federal contracting certifications and support for government procurement opportunities
Regular educational events and training workshops for entrepreneurs (marketing, business planning)
Disaster recovery assistance for businesses affected by emergencies
Documented track record of successful financing for manufacturing, food service, and tech businesses
cons: Does not directly originate or fund loans—borrowers must qualify with third-party SBA-approved lenders
Limited office hours (8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. weekdays only) and closed on federal holidays
Serves only San Diego and Imperial counties—geographic limitation for broader regional businesses
SBA loan approvals still subject to standard underwriting requirements and may take longer than alternative lenders
No online loan application or funding capability—requires in-person consultation or phone contact
best_for: Small business owners in San Diego or Imperial counties seeking affordable government-backed financing
Veteran-owned businesses looking for specialized SBA support and training
Manufacturers and food service businesses needing equipment or facility expansion loans
Entrepreneurs seeking free counseling and connections to vetted lenders before applying for capital
diagnosis: Best for small business owners in San Diego or Imperial counties who need affordable, government-backed financing options and free business counseling. Primary caveat: the SBA is a government intermediary, not a direct lender—actual loans come from third-party SBA-approved lenders, and approval still requires meeting standard underwriting criteria and takes longer than alternative funding sources.
services: SBA 504 loan program connections (for equipment and facility financing)
Federal contracting certifications and procurement support
Disaster recovery assistance and emergency business support
Connections to SBA-approved lenders in the region
Referrals to partner organizations and community groups
Marketing and business planning educational workshops
Veteran-owned business support and specialized training
Small business registration guidance and state/federal resource navigation
Email alerts with free small business tips and event notifications
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 550 West C St # 550, San Diego, CA 92101
